name: user-profile
observations: Building AI assistant frameworks and tools for developers
Values: practical over philosophical, right-sized complexity, invisible workflows
Works across: .NET APIs, Blazor, MAUI, Unity, MCP servers

name: workflow-conventions
observations: Follow structured workflow phases (Triage → Discover → Plan → Build → Document) for non-trivial tasks
Skip phases only when the task is clearly simple enough to not need them
Jumping straight to coding without understanding the codebase leads to rework
When a task involves more than a single-file edit, start with at least a brief discovery phase

name: framework-design-principles
observations: Skills should be invisible — a good workflow doesn't feel like a workflow
Auto-discovery over registration — found by convention (SKILL.md presence) not by maintaining lists
Orchestrators above sub-skills — glue skills should have higher trigger priority
Read-only agents stay read-only — safety model depends on tool restrictions, not instruction text
